title: oxalis hirta var. tubiflora
intro: Oxalis hirta var. tubiflora, also known as the hairy woodsorrel, is a plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. It is a perennial herb with leaves that have a sour taste due to the presence of oxalic acid, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including arthritis, fever, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Oxalis hirta var. tubiflora can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
